quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 12, Biology I
quiz.subtitle: Bioenergetics: Citric Acid Cycle, Metabolic Reactions, and Enzyme Specifics
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Krebs Cycle Products, Oxaloacetate Regeneration, Isomerization, Phosphorylation, Kinase Enzymes, Glycolysis Location

questions.question: How many molecules of carbon dioxide (CO₂) are produced during one turn of the citric acid cycle?
questions.explanation: One turn of the citric acid cycle produces 2 molecules of CO₂. These are released when isocitrate is converted to α-ketoglutarate and when α-ketoglutarate is converted to succinyl-CoA. Since two turns occur per glucose (one per each acetyl-CoA), a total of 4 CO₂ are produced per glucose from the Krebs cycle.

questions.question: Which type of metabolic reaction does not alter the atomic composition of the molecule involved?
questions.explanation: Isomerization rearranges the structure of a molecule but does not change its atomic composition (formula). For example, converting glucose-6-phosphate to fructose-6-phosphate changes the arrangement of atoms but both molecules are C₆H₁₁O₆P. Oxidation/reduction involve electron transfer, and phosphorylation adds a phosphate group.

questions.question: How many molecules of ATP (or GTP) are directly produced from one turn of the citric acid cycle?
questions.explanation: One turn of the citric acid cycle produces 1 ATP (or GTP) via substrate-level phosphorylation when succinyl-CoA is converted to succinate. Since each glucose produces 2 acetyl-CoA molecules, the cycle turns twice per glucose, yielding 2 ATP directly from the Krebs cycle.

questions.question: Which type of metabolic reaction involves the transfer of a phosphate group to a molecule?
questions.explanation: Phosphorylation is the addition of a phosphate group (PO₄³⁻) to a molecule. Examples include: ADP + Pi → ATP (phosphorylation of ADP), and glucose → glucose-6-phosphate (phosphorylation of glucose). Kinases are enzymes that catalyze phosphorylation reactions.

questions.question: Which molecule is regenerated at the end of the citric acid cycle and is also used in the first step of the cycle?
questions.explanation: Oxaloacetate (4-carbon) is regenerated at the end of each turn of the Krebs cycle when malate is oxidized. It then combines with a new acetyl-CoA (2-carbon) to form citrate (6-carbon), starting the next turn. This regeneration is what makes it a 'cycle'.

questions.question: Which of the following terms does not accurately describe the glycolytic pathway?
questions.explanation: Glycolysis is a catabolic pathway (breaks down glucose into pyruvate), not anabolic (which builds molecules). It is metabolic (involved in chemical processes) and chemotrophic (involves chemical energy transformation). Anabolic pathways include gluconeogenesis, protein synthesis, and fatty acid synthesis.

questions.question: How many carbon atoms are present in two molecules of glucose?
questions.explanation: Glucose has the molecular formula C₆H₁₂O₆, meaning each glucose molecule contains 6 carbon atoms. Two glucose molecules contain 2 × 6 = 12 carbon atoms total.

questions.question: Which of the following is not true of the location of the electron transport system?
options: It may be found in mitochondria in animal cells.
It may be found in the cellular membrane of insects.
It may be found in chloroplasts in plant cells.
It may be found in the plasma membrane of bacteria.
questions.explanation: The electron transport system is NOT found in the 'cellular membrane of insects.' In animal cells (including insects), the ETC is located in the inner mitochondrial membrane, not the cellular (plasma) membrane. In plant cells, ETCs are found in both mitochondria (respiration) and chloroplasts (photosynthesis). In bacteria, which lack mitochondria, the ETC is located in the plasma membrane.

questions.question: Which isn't an end product of glycolysis?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide (CO₂) is not an end product of glycolysis. Glycolysis produces 2 pyruvate, 2 NADH, and a net gain of 2 ATP. CO₂ is released later during pyruvate decarboxylation (conversion of pyruvate to acetyl-CoA) and during the citric acid cycle.

questions.question: Which of the following atoms is involved in glycolytic reactions to help stabilize or shield negative charges?
questions.explanation: Magnesium ions (Mg²⁺) play a crucial role in glycolysis by binding to ATP and intermediates. ATP typically exists as a complex with Mg²⁺, which shields the negative charges on the phosphate groups, making it easier for enzymes like hexokinase and phosphofructokinase to transfer phosphate groups. Magnesium acts as a cofactor for several glycolytic enzymes.

questions.question: Which of the following metabolic processes generates the most ATP?
questions.explanation: The Electron Transport Chain (ETC) generates the most ATP (approximately 32-34 ATP per glucose) through chemiosmosis. However, note that oxidative phosphorylation is the overall process that includes both the ETC and ATP synthesis via chemiosmosis. Glycolysis yields 2 ATP (net), and the Citric Acid Cycle yields 2 ATP (or GTP) directly per glucose, plus electron carriers that feed into the ETC.

questions.question: What is the end product of aerobic glycolysis?
questions.explanation: The end product of glycolysis is pyruvate (3-carbon molecule). ATP and NADH are also produced during glycolysis, but they are co-products, not the final organic molecule product. Pyruvate then enters the mitochondria for further oxidation.

questions.question: In which part of the cell does glycolysis occur?
questions.explanation: Glycolysis occurs in the cytosol (cytoplasm) of the cell. It does not require any membrane-bound organelles. The pyruvate produced then enters mitochondria for the Krebs cycle and oxidative phosphorylation.

questions.question: An enzyme that catalyzes the addition of a phosphate group to a molecule is called:
questions.explanation: Kinases are enzymes that catalyze the transfer of a phosphate group from ATP to a substrate (phosphorylation). Examples: hexokinase (phosphorylates glucose), phosphofructokinase (phosphorylates fructose-6-phosphate). Phosphorylases add phosphate using inorganic phosphate, not ATP.

questions.question: Which of the following molecules is neither oxidized nor reduced during electron transport in the electron transport chain (ETC)?
questions.explanation: Coenzyme A (CoA) is not involved in electron transport. Its role is to carry acetyl groups (as acetyl-CoA) into the Krebs cycle. NADH and FADH₂ are oxidized (donate electrons), and oxygen is reduced (accepts electrons) in the ETC.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered the details of the Krebs cycle and metabolic reaction types.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Review the difference between anabolic and catabolic pathways.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on what happens in each turn of the citric acid cycle.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the Krebs cycle products and the location of different metabolic processes.
